title: Episode 244: Kona Series- Mirinda Carfrae and Craig Alexander
summary: 🎧 KONA DAILY POD – DAY 3 | PRESENTED BY TRAININGPEAKS Welcome back to The Kona Daily Pod, your front-row seat to the Ironman World Championship 2025 with the Pro Tri News crew — brought to you by TrainingPeaks, the number one training app in the world. Today, hosts Talbot Cox, Matt Lieto, Kaden Lieto, and Mark Matthews are joined by triathlon royalty — Mirinda Carfrae and Craig Alexander — to dive into what it takes to master the Kona run course. Both world champions share stories of racing down Ali‘i Drive, managing heat, and executing the perfect marathon after 180 kilometers on the bike. From pacing and nutrition to the mental game that defines Kona, these two legends explain how the race is truly won on the run. 💬 Topics in this episode: – Mirinda Carfrae’s mindset and pacing through the Energy Lab – Craig Alexander’s strategy for building speed off the bike – How the Kona run has evolved with faster, more aggressive racing – Mental toughness, cooling tactics, and adapting to conditions – Using TrainingPeaks data to dial in pacing and recovery – Reflections on iconic finishes and what separates champions 💪 Presented By TrainingPeaks-- Special Offer from Training Peaks  And TrainingPeaks has an exclusive offer for Pro Tri News listeners. title: Episode 243: Kona Series- Craig Alexander
summary: 🎧 KONA DAILY POD – DAY 2 | PRESENTED BY TRAININGPEAKS Welcome back to The Kona Daily Pod, your inside look at the Ironman World Championship 2025 with the Pro Tri News crew — brought to you by TrainingPeaks, the number one training app in the world. Today, hosts Talbot Cox, Mark Matthews, Matt Lieto, and Kaiden Lieto sit down with three-time Ironman World Champion Craig "Crowie" Alexander to talk everything bike course and race strategy in Kona. Craig shares how the Hawi winds, nutrition timing, and power management define the race — and why today’s generation of pros ride smarter than ever. The crew also dives into equipment choices, aero setups, and how data tools like TrainingPeaks have changed the way athletes build their race plans. 💬 Topics in this episode: – Craig Alexander’s insights on the Kona bike course and modern race tactics – Handling the crosswinds and heat through the Queen K – Balancing power output and fueling strategy – Evolution of bike technology and aero efficiency – How TrainingPeaks transformed race preparation and data analysis – Predictions for the fastest bike splits in 2025 These are short daily pods raw, real, and straight from the island. title: Episode 236: Should 70.3 Be in the Olympics
